text: The obstacle in the path becomes the path. Never forget, within every obstacle is an opportunity to improve our condition.
author: Ryan Holiday

text: First, solve the problem. Then, write the code.
author: John Johnson

text: Give me six hours to chop down a tree and I will spend the first four sharpening the axe.
author: Abraham Lincoln

text: It is not the mountain we conquer, but ourselves.
author: Sir Edmund Hillary

text: Strategy without tactics is the slowest route to victory. Tactics without strategy is the noise before defeat.
author: Sun Tzu

text: You do not rise to the level of your goals. You fall to the level of your systems.
author: James Clear

text: Simplicity is the soul of efficiency.
author: Austin Freeman

text: We suffer more often in imagination than in reality.
author: Seneca

text: Somewhere between the bottom of the climb and the summit is the answer to the mystery why we climb.
author: Greg Child

text: In theory, there is no difference between theory and practice. But, in practice, there is.
author: Jan L. A. van de Snepscheut

text: The only way to do great work is to love what you do.
author: Steve Jobs

text: Amateurs sit and wait for inspiration, the rest of us just get up and go to work.
author: Stephen King

text: The best time to plant a tree was 20 years ago. The second best time is now.
author: Chinese Proverb

text: Make it work, make it right, make it fast.
author: Kent Beck

text: He who has a why to live for can bear almost any how.
author: Friedrich Nietzsche

text: A journey of a thousand miles begins with a single step.
author: Lao Tzu

text: Do not seek to follow in the footsteps of the men of old; seek what they sought.
author: Matsuo Basho

text: The supreme art of war is to subdue the enemy without fighting.
author: Sun Tzu

text: The hardest mountain to climb is the one within.
author: J. Lynn

text: Perfection is achieved not when there is nothing more to add, but rather when there is nothing more to take away.
author: Antoine de Saint-Exupery

text: Talk is cheap. Show me the code.
author: Linus Torvalds

text: To be calm is the highest achievement of the self.
author: Zen Proverb

text: Life is what happens when you're busy making other plans.
author: John Lennon

text: The man who moves a mountain begins by carrying away small stones.
author: Confucius

text: Success is not final, failure is not fatal: it is the courage to continue that counts.
author: Winston Churchill

text: Programs must be written for people to read, and only incidentally for machines to execute.
author: Harold Abelson

text: What lies behind us and what lies before us are tiny matters compared to what lies within us.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

text: Knowing is not enough; we must apply. Willing is not enough; we must do.
author: Johann Wolfgang von Goethe

text: Mountains are not stadiums where I satisfy my ambition to achieve, they are the cathedrals where I practice my religion.
author: Anatoli Boukreev

text: In the midst of chaos, there is also opportunity.
author: Sun Tzu

text: Quality is a product of a conflict between programmers and testers.
author: Yegor Bugayenko

text: The only true wisdom is in knowing you know nothing.
author: Socrates

text: Believe you can and you're halfway there.
author: Theodore Roosevelt

text: May your trails be crooked, winding, lonesome, dangerous, leading to the most amazing view.
author: Edward Abbey

text: The most important investments you make are in yourself.
author: Warren Buffett

text: It always seems impossible until it's done.
author: Nelson Mandela

text: Truth can only be found in one place: the code.
author: Robert C. Martin

text: I have not failed. I've just found 10,000 ways that won't work.
author: Thomas Edison

text: Nature does not hurry, yet everything is accomplished.
author: Lao Tzu

text: If you are going through hell, keep going.
author: Winston Churchill

text: Code is like humor. When you have to explain it, it’s bad.
author: Cory House

text: The mind is everything. What you think you become.
author: Buddha

text: Your time is limited, don't waste it living someone else's life.
author: Steve Jobs

text: Earth and sky, woods and fields, lakes and rivers, the mountain and the sea, are excellent schoolmasters.
author: John Lubbock

text: To improve is to change; to be perfect is to change often.
author: Winston Churchill

text: Any fool can write code that a computer can understand. Good programmers write code that humans can understand.
author: Martin Fowler

text: Happiness is not something ready made. It comes from your own actions.
author: Dalai Lama

text: The unexamined life is not worth living.
author: Socrates

text: Every mountain top is within reach if you just keep climbing.
author: Barry Finlay

text: Experience is the name everyone gives to their mistakes.
author: Oscar Wilde
